guidelines.title: Antiepileptic Drug Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIEPILEPTIC DRUG (AED) DOSING GUIDELINES

FIRST-LINE AGENTS:

Levetiracetam (Keppra):
- Initial: 500 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 500 mg twice daily every 2 weeks
- Target: 1000-1500 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 3000 mg/day
- Renal adjustment: CrCl <80 requires dose reduction
- Side effects: Irritability, behavioral changes

Lamotrigine (Lamictal):
- Without valproate: Start 25 mg daily x2 weeks, then 50 mg daily x2 weeks, increase by 50 mg/day q1-2 weeks
- With valproate: Start 25 mg every other day x2 weeks, then 25 mg daily, slow titration
- Target: 100-400 mg/day (200 mg/day with valproate)
- Maximum: 600 mg/day (200 mg/day with valproate)
- CRITICAL: Slow titration to prevent Stevens-Johnson syndrome

Valproate (Depakote):
- Initial: 10-15 mg/kg/day in 2-3 divided doses
- Titration: Increase by 5-10 mg/kg/day weekly
- Target: 20-60 mg/kg/day
- Therapeutic level: 50-100 mcg/mL
- Maximum: 60 mg/kg/day
- Avoid in pregnancy (teratogenic), women of childbearing potential

Carbamazepine (Tegretol):
- Initial: 200 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 200 mg/day weekly
- Target: 800-1200 mg/day in divided doses
- Therapeutic level: 4-12 mcg/mL
- Auto-induces metabolism (levels drop after 3-5 weeks)
- HLA-B*1502 testing in Asian patients (SJS risk)

Oxcarbazepine (Trileptal):
- Initial: 300 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 300-600 mg/day weekly
- Target: 600-1200 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 2400 mg/day
- 25-30% cross-reactivity with carbamazepine allergy
- Monitor sodium (hyponatremia risk)

Phenytoin (Dilantin):
- Loading: 15-20 mg/kg IV (max rate 50 mg/min) or PO in 3 divided doses
- Maintenance: 5-7 mg/kg/day, usually 300-400 mg/day
- Therapeutic level: 10-20 mcg/mL (total), 1-2 mcg/mL (free)
- Nonlinear kinetics: Small dose changes = large level changes
- Monitor for gingival hyperplasia, hirsutism

SECOND-LINE/ADJUNCTIVE AGENTS:

Topiramate (Topamax):
- Initial: 25 mg daily
- Titration: Increase by 25-50 mg/day weekly
- Target: 100-200 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 400 mg/day (up to 1600 mg in some cases)
- Side effects: Cognitive dulling, word-finding difficulty, weight loss, kidney stones

Zonisamide (Zonegran):
- Initial: 100 mg daily
- Titration: Increase by 100 mg/day every 2 weeks
- Target: 200-400 mg daily
- Maximum: 600 mg/day
- Sulfonamide allergy cross-reactivity

Lacosamide (Vimpat):
- Initial: 50 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 50 mg twice daily weekly
- Target: 100-200 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 400 mg/day
- Monitor PR interval (can cause AV block)

Brivaracetam (Briviact):
- Initial: 50 mg twice daily (can start at target dose)
- Target: 50-100 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 200 mg/day
- Less behavioral side effects than levetiracetam

Clobazam (Onfi):
- Initial: 5-10 mg daily
- Titration: Increase by 5-10 mg weekly
- Target: 20-40 mg/day in 2 divided doses
- Maximum: 40 mg/day (20 mg/day for poor CYP2C19 metabolizers)

Perampanel (Fycompa):
- Initial: 2 mg at bedtime
- Titration: Increase by 2 mg weekly
- Target: 4-12 mg at bedtime
- Maximum: 12 mg/day
- REMS program: Aggression, hostility risk

STATUS EPILEPTICUS PROTOCOL:

First-line (0-5 minutes):
- Lorazepam 0.1 mg/kg IV (max 4 mg per dose), may repeat once
- OR Midazolam 10 mg IM (if no IV access)
- OR Diazepam 0.15-0.2 mg/kg IV (max 10 mg)

Second-line (5-20 minutes):
- Fosphenytoin 20 mg PE/kg IV (max rate 150 mg PE/min)
- OR Valproate 40 mg/kg IV (max rate 10 mg/kg/min)
- OR Levetiracetam 60 mg/kg IV (max 4500 mg)

Third-line (Refractory SE):
- Midazolam infusion: 0.2 mg/kg bolus, then 0.1-2 mg/kg/hour
- OR Propofol: 1-2 mg/kg bolus, then 1-15 mg/kg/hour
- OR Pentobarbital: 5-15 mg/kg bolus, then 0.5-5 mg/kg/hour
- Target: EEG burst suppression

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (AAN guidelines, ILAE recommendations)

guidelines.title: Acute Stroke Treatment Protocol
guidelines.content:
ACUTE ISCHEMIC STROKE TREATMENT PROTOCOL

IV THROMBOLYSIS (Alteplase/tPA):

Eligibility:
- Symptom onset <4.5 hours (3 hours for extended criteria)
- Measurable neurological deficit
- No contraindications

Dosing:
- Total dose: 0.9 mg/kg (maximum 90 mg)
- 10% as IV bolus over 1 minute
- Remaining 90% as IV infusion over 60 minutes

Example (70 kg patient):
- Total: 63 mg
- Bolus: 6.3 mg over 1 minute
- Infusion: 56.7 mg over 60 minutes

TENECTEPLASE (Alternative):
- Dose: 0.25 mg/kg IV bolus (max 25 mg)
- Single bolus administration
- Non-inferior to alteplase in recent trials
- Easier to administer

Blood Pressure Management with tPA:
- Pre-treatment: Must be <185/110 mmHg
- During/After treatment: Maintain <180/105 mmHg x24 hours

Labetalol:
- 10-20 mg IV over 1-2 min, may repeat or double q10min
- Max: 300 mg
- OR Infusion 2-8 mg/min

Nicardipine:
- Start 5 mg/hour IV, increase by 2.5 mg/hour q5min
- Max: 15 mg/hour

Clevidipine:
- Start 1-2 mg/hour IV, double q90sec until goal
- Max: 21 mg/hour

MECHANICAL THROMBECTOMY:
- Large vessel occlusion (ICA, M1, sometimes M2)
- Within 24 hours (with appropriate imaging selection)
- NIHSS ≥6 typically
- ASPECTS ≥6
- Can be done with or after tPA

ANTIPLATELET THERAPY:

After tPA:
- Wait 24 hours, repeat CT to exclude hemorrhage
- Then aspirin 325 mg

No tPA given:
- Aspirin 325 mg within 24-48 hours of onset
- OR Aspirin 81 mg + Clopidogrel 75 mg for minor stroke/TIA (DAPT x21 days)

Secondary Prevention:
- Aspirin 81-325 mg daily
- OR Clopidogrel 75 mg daily (if aspirin allergic)
- OR Aspirin 25 mg + Dipyridamole ER 200 mg twice daily

STATIN THERAPY:
- High-intensity statin for all ischemic stroke
- Atorvastatin 40-80 mg daily
- OR Rosuvastatin 20-40 mg daily
- Target LDL <70 mg/dL (some guidelines <55)

BLOOD PRESSURE MANAGEMENT (After acute phase):
- Target: <130/80 mmHg
- Start after 24-48 hours if stable
- ACE-I + thiazide diuretic preferred (PROGRESS trial)

GLUCOSE MANAGEMENT:
- Target: 140-180 mg/dL during acute phase
- Treat hypoglycemia immediately (<60 mg/dL)
- Avoid hyperglycemia (>180 mg/dL)

INTRACEREBRAL HEMORRHAGE (ICH):

Blood Pressure:
- Target SBP <140 mmHg if presenting SBP 150-220
- Reduce within 1 hour

Reversal of Anticoagulation:

Warfarin:
- 4-Factor PCC: 25-50 units/kg based on INR
- Plus Vitamin K 10 mg IV
- Target INR <1.5 within 4 hours

DOACs:
- Dabigatran: Idarucizumab 5 g IV
- Rivaroxaban/Apixaban: Andexanet alfa or 4F-PCC 50 units/kg
- Consider activated charcoal if ingestion <2 hours

DVT PROPHYLAXIS:
- Intermittent pneumatic compression immediately
- Pharmacological (LMWH or heparin) after 24-48 hours if stable hemorrhage

SEIZURE PROPHYLAXIS:
- NOT routinely recommended for ischemic stroke
- Consider for ICH with lobar involvement
- Levetiracetam 500-1000 mg q12h if needed

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (AHA/ASA Stroke Guidelines)

guidelines.title: Migraine Treatment and Prevention Guidelines
guidelines.content:
MIGRAINE TREATMENT AND PREVENTION GUIDELINES

ACUTE TREATMENT:

TRIPTANS (First-line for moderate-severe):

Sumatriptan:
- Oral: 25, 50, or 100 mg (start 50 mg)
- Nasal spray: 20 mg
- Subcutaneous: 6 mg (fastest onset, 10-15 min)
- May repeat after 2 hours (max 200 mg PO/day, 12 mg SC/day)

Rizatriptan (Maxalt):
- 5 or 10 mg PO (use 5 mg with propranolol)
- ODT available for rapid dissolution
- May repeat after 2 hours (max 30 mg/day)

Electriptan (Relpax):
- 20 or 40 mg PO
- May repeat after 2 hours (max 80 mg/day)
- Higher efficacy, longer half-life

Zolmitriptan (Zomig):
- 2.5 or 5 mg PO
- Nasal spray: 2.5 or 5 mg
- Max 10 mg/day

Naratriptan (Amerge):
- 1 or 2.5 mg PO
- Slower onset but longer duration
- Better for menstrual migraine
- Max 5 mg/day

Frovatriptan (Frova):
- 2.5 mg PO
- Longest half-life (26 hours)
- Excellent for menstrual migraine prevention
- Max 7.5 mg/day

Almotriptan (Axert):
- 6.25 or 12.5 mg PO
- FDA approved for adolescents
- Max 25 mg/day

CGRP ANTAGONISTS (Gepants):

Ubrogepant (Ubrelvy):
- 50 or 100 mg PO
- May repeat after 2 hours (max 200 mg/day)
- Safe with cardiovascular disease (no vasoconstriction)

Rimegepant (Nurtec ODT):
- 75 mg ODT
- Single dose per 24 hours
- Also approved for prevention (every other day)

NSAIDs:
- Ibuprofen 400-800 mg
- Naproxen 500-750 mg
- Ketorolac 30-60 mg IM/IV
- Diclofenac potassium 50 mg

COMBINATION ANALGESICS:
- Excedrin (acetaminophen 250 mg + aspirin 250 mg + caffeine 65 mg)
- 2 tablets, max 8 tablets/day

ANTIEMETICS:
- Metoclopramide 10 mg IV/PO
- Prochlorperazine 10 mg IV/PO
- Ondansetron 4-8 mg IV/PO

PREVENTIVE TREATMENT (≥4 headache days/month):

BETA-BLOCKERS:

Propranolol:
- Start 40 mg twice daily
- Target 80-240 mg/day
- Level A evidence

Metoprolol:
- Start 50 mg twice daily
- Target 100-200 mg/day

ANTIDEPRESSANTS:

Amitriptyline:
- Start 10-25 mg at bedtime
- Titrate to 50-150 mg at bedtime
- Level A evidence

Venlafaxine:
- Start 37.5 mg daily
- Target 75-150 mg daily

ANTIEPILEPTICS:

Topiramate:
- Start 25 mg at bedtime
- Titrate by 25 mg weekly
- Target 50-100 mg twice daily
- Level A evidence

Valproate:
- 500-1500 mg/day in divided doses
- Level A evidence
- Avoid in women of childbearing potential

CGRP MONOCLONAL ANTIBODIES:

Erenumab (Aimovig):
- 70 mg SC monthly (may increase to 140 mg)
- First FDA-approved CGRP mAb

Fremanezumab (Ajovy):
- 225 mg SC monthly OR 675 mg quarterly

Galcanezumab (Emgality):
- Loading: 240 mg SC (two 120 mg injections)
- Maintenance: 120 mg SC monthly

Eptinezumab (Vyepti):
- 100 mg IV every 3 months
- May increase to 300 mg

ATOGEPANT (Qulipta):
- 10, 30, or 60 mg once daily
- First oral CGRP antagonist for prevention

ONABOTULINUMTOXINA (for chronic migraine ≥15 days/month):
- 155-195 units divided across 31-39 injection sites
- Every 12 weeks
- Specific injection protocol (frontalis, corrugator, procerus, temporalis, occipitalis, cervical paraspinal, trapezius)

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (AAN/AHS guidelines)
